name: Circuit from lake Uby
name_original: Circuit depuis le lac de l'Uby
region_intro: Road cycling around Cazaubon, located in the Gers department of Occitanie, France, features a landscape of rolling hills and verdant valleys. The region is characterized by its "Little Tuscany" feel, with expansive vineyards and agricultural lands. Road cyclists navigate mostly paved surfaces, encountering gentle elevation changes and scenic views. Lac de l'Uby provides a central natural feature, and on clear days, distant views of the Pyrenees mountains are visible. Best road cycling routes around Cazaubon The most popular road cycling route is Circuit from lake Uby, a 19.4 miles (31.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of Lac de l'Uby and the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Labastide-d'Armagnac – Our Lady of Cyclists loop from Barbotan-les-Thermes, a moderate 41.9 miles (67.4 km) path. This route traverses the Gascon countryside, passing through vineyards and small villages. Local road cyclists also love the Cazaubon – Labastide-d'Armagnac loop from Barbotan-les-Thermes, a 36.1 miles (58.1 km) trail leading through rolling hills and agricultural land, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Road cycling around Cazaubon is defined by rolling countryside, vineyards, and the presence of Lac de l'Uby. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more demanding journeys with significant climbing. The routes in Cazaubon are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 150 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cazaubon's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Cazaubon?
A: Cazaubon offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 20 different tours available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy loops to more challenging journeys with significant climbing.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Cazaubon?
A: The terrain around Cazaubon is characterized by rolling hills and verdant valleys, often described as having a 'Little Tuscany' feel. Cyclists will primarily navigate mostly paved surfaces, passing through expansive vineyards and agricultural lands. On clear days, you might even catch distant views of the Pyrenees mountains.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Cazaubon has options for all fitness levels. For an easier ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962115379/circuit-from-lake-uby" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circuit from Lake Uby&lt;/a&gt;, which is 19.4 miles (31.2 km) long and offers gentle elevation changes with scenic views of Lac de l'Uby. Another accessible option is the 'Bench With a View of the Lake – Lake Uby loop from Cazaubon' mentioned in the region research, which is great for all fitness levels with mostly paved surfaces.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see while cycling in Cazaubon?
A: Road cycling routes in Cazaubon often feature beautiful natural landscapes. You'll frequently encounter the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4808798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de l'Uby&lt;/a&gt;, a central hub for various activities. The routes also wind through picturesque rolling countryside and vineyards, offering a peaceful environment. On clear days, the distant skyline of the Pyrenees mountains can be glimpsed.

Q: Are there any historical sites or villages to explore along the routes?
A: Yes, the region around Cazaubon is rich in history and culture. Many routes pass through charming Gascon countryside and small villages. You might encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/676194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Labastide-d'Armagnac&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3510388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eauze&lt;/a&gt;. Cazaubon itself boasts a medieval fortified village with ancient ramparts, and there's a self-drive route to discover its seven churches, some dating back to the Middle Ages.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Cazaubon?
A: Cazaubon's pleasant climate makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. The region's 'Little Tuscany' feel with verdant valleys and vineyards is particularly enjoyable during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild. Summer also offers great conditions, though it can be warmer.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Cazaubon?
A: Absolutely. While many routes are suitable for various fitness levels, the region also offers unique family-friendly experiences. For instance, the Vélorail de l'Armagnac near Nogaro allows families to pedal along a disused railway line, offering a different way to experience the countryside. For traditional road cycling, look for routes with minimal elevation gain and mostly paved surfaces, such as the 'Bench With a View of the Lake – Lake Uby loop from Cazaubon'.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Cazaubon?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars. Reviewers often praise the peaceful environment, the scenic views of rolling countryside and vineyards, and the variety of routes that cater to different abilities. The presence of Lac de l'Uby as a central feature is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any longer road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for more demanding journeys, you can find routes that involve significant climbing and cover longer distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26266447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Labastide-d'Armagnac – Our Lady of Cyclists loop from Barbotan-les-Thermes&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 41.9 miles (67.4 km) path that traverses the Gascon countryside, passing through vineyards and small villages. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37733429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cazaubon – Our Lady of Cyclists loop from Barbotan-les-Thermes&lt;/a&gt;, which is 77.1 km long.

Q: Are there amenities like bike rentals available in Cazaubon?
A: Yes, Cazaubon is well-equipped for cyclists. The Office Municipal de Tourisme et du Thermalisme offers bike rental services, including mountain bikes (MTB), all-terrain bikes (VTC), electric-assist bikes (VAE), and children's bikes. This makes it convenient to explore the area even if you don't bring your own equipment.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in the Cazaubon area?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Cazaubon are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962115379/circuit-from-lake-uby" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circuit from Lake Uby&lt;/a&gt;, the 'Duck Farm loop from Cazaubon', and the 'Jean Bartherotte Arena – Cazaubon loop from Barbotan-les-Thermes', providing convenient options for exploration.
tips: A fantastic nature reserve with trails around its shores makes this lake a fantastic place for hiking.
